Items.Title: 向我介绍 PHP | Pitch me on PHP
Items.Description:
本·哈尔彭

发表于新系列的一部分！ 欢迎参与并权衡过去的问题。

假设我以前从未使用过 PHP。 谁能给出该语言的功能以及您为什么喜欢它的原因？ 也可以随意谈谈缺点。

阿纳布罗伊乔杜里—— 马塞洛·巴雷托·尼斯 - Apr 22 4月22日



原文:

Ben Halpern

Posted on May 14

1 Pitch me on Python 2 Pitch me on PHP

Part of a new series! Feel welcome to dip in and weigh in on a past question.

Let's say I've never used PHP before. Can anyone give the run down of what the language does and why you prefer it? Feel free to touch on drawbacks as well.

arnabroychowdhury - May 11

Mohammed Nadeem Shareef - Apr 22

Marcelo Barreto Nees - Apr 22
Items.Author: ben

Items.Title: 让开发人员成为生产力绝地的 7 个简单技巧 ⚡️ | 7 Easy Hacks for Developers to become a Productivity Jedi ⚡️
Items.Description:
每个人都想用最少的努力完成很多事情。 这篇文章包含 7 个这样久经考验的技巧，可以帮助您在少工作的同时取得更多成就！

我们生活在一个信息丰富的世界里，使用谷歌可以在 1 分钟内回答大多数问题，因此试图记住所有内容是没有意义的。

你唯一应该背诵的东西是编程的核心概念，没有它你就不会知道你不知道什么。

例如：如果您尝试重复搜索元素，知道哈希列表将导致快速搜索，这对您有很大帮助。 如何实际使用哈希列表因语言而异，但可以在Google上轻松查找

hash_list = set ( our_list ) for search_element in search_list : if search_element in hash_list : pass # handle element

时间盒是一种将时间分配给特定任务的技术。 这可以让你做深入的工作。 请记住在开始工作之前将手机保持静音。

 8:30 --> 9:00 Emails & Catch up on Slack 9:00 --> 13:00 Working on XYZ project 13:00 --> 14:00 Lunch and break 14:00 --> 15:00 Team meeting 15:00 --> 16:30 Working on XYZ project

如果你的工作是吃青蛙，最好早上第一件事。 如果你必须吃两只青蛙，从较大的一只开始。

老实说，没有人喜欢吃青蛙，即使是比喻性的。 但是，在一天的早些时候完成待办事项中的艰巨任务有一个巨大的优势：你放弃了无法完成任务的感觉（这会慢慢吞噬你的灵魂），换取随之而来的剧烈行动痛苦当天晚些时候令人难以置信的骄傲！

能量水平全天变化。 花几天时间逐小时记录您的能量水平，并制定适合您身体的时间表。

与你的身体一起工作而不是对抗它的结果，对于大多数人来说都是惊人的，而且生产力将简直飞涨！

它提倡通过重复使用和回收您已经完成的工作来节省时间。

重复本质上是低效的。 这种方法的真正强大之处在于，它不仅限于编码，还可以应用于电子邮件、自动支付卡等等！

研究表明，大约 2% 的人可以有效地执行多项任务。 每个人都认为他们是神话般的百分之二的人是令人难以置信的。

虽然多任务处理并没有真正同时处理多个任务，但实际上你在多个任务之间不断切换，错过了深度工作，并且有效地完成了所有这些工作。

每天完成您的每日目标，您都会在日历中添加一个❌。

承诺的最初几天通常是最难的——但你做出改变的动力会让你度过难关。

随着链条越来越长，不打破你的连胜成为你想要退出的时代的首要任务，从而改变生活！

这就是所有的人！ 现在，您拥有成为生产力之神的工具！

世界等你来拿！

需要顶级前端开发自由职业者来消除您的开发困境？ 在Upwork上联系我

想看看我在做什么？ 查看我的个人网站和GitHub

想要连接？ 在LinkedIn上联系我

我是一名自由职业者，将于 2022 年年中开始担任Digital Nomad 。 想赶上旅程吗？ 在Instagram 上关注我

关注我的博客，了解Dev上的每周新花絮

常问问题

这些是我得到的一些常见问题。 所以，我希望这个常见问题解答部分能解决您的问题。



原文:

Everyone wants to get a lot done while putting in the least amount of effort. This article contains 7 such tried & tested hacks that will help you achieve more while working less!

1. Don't Memorize Google-able info!🔎

We live in a world of abundant information, and most questions can be answered within 1 minute using Google, so there is no point in trying to memorize everything.

The only things you should be learning by heart are the core concepts of programming, without which you would not know what you don't know.

Eg: If you are trying to repeatedly search for elements, knowing a hash list will result in blazing fast searches that will help you a lot. How to actually use a hash list varies from language to language, but can easily be looked up on Google

hash_list = set(our_list) for search_element in search_list: if search_element in hash_list: pass # handle element

2. Use Time Boxing ⌛

Time Boxing is the technique of blocking out & allocating time to a specific task. This allows you to do deep work. Just remember to keep your phone on silent before you start working.

8:30 --> 9:00
Emails & Catch up on Slack

9:00 --> 13:00
Working on XYZ project

13:00 --> 14:00
Lunch and break

14:00 --> 15:00
Team meeting

15:00 --> 16:30
Working on XYZ project

3. Eat the damn Frog! 🐸

If it's your job to eat the frog, best to do it first thing in the morning. If you have to eat two frogs, start with the larger one.

Let's be honest, nobody likes eating frogs, even metaphorical ones. But getting the hard tasks on your to-do done early in the day has a huge advantage: you trade the feeling of being someone who can't get things done (that slowly eats away your soul), for an acute pain of action followed by incredible pride later in the day!

4. Use Biology to your Advantage! 📈

The energy levels vary throughout the day. Take a few days to document your energy levels hour by hour and create a schedule suited to your body.

The results of working with your body instead of against it, will astounding for most people & the productivity will simply go through the roof!

5. Stay DRY! 🏜️

The DRY (Don’t Repeat Yourself) principle is one of the core principles of Programming. It advocates saving time by reusing and recycling work you’ve already done.

Repetition is by its nature inefficient. The DRY Principle encourages you to create workflows and templates to minimize them. The true power of this method comes from the fact, that it is not restricted only to coding, but can be applied to emails, automating your card payments, and much more!

6. Forget Multi-Tasking! 🤹

Research states around two percent of people can multitask effectively. How everyone believes that they are the mythical two percenter is mind-boggling.

While multi-tasking you are not truly handling multiple tasks at the same time, you are actually constantly switching between multiple tasks, missing out on deep work, and effectively doing a mediocre job at all of them.

7. Make the Chain Longer! 🔗

The “Don't Break The Chain” method commits you to complete a daily goal for an extended period. Each day that you complete your daily goal, you add an ❌ to a calendar.

The first few days of commitment are usually the hardest – but your motivation to make a change pulls you through.

As the chain grows longer, not breaking your streak becomes the priority that pulls you through the times when you want to quit, resulting in life-changing improvements!

That's all folks! Now you have the tools in your arsenal to become a productivity god!

The world is up for your taking!

Want to see what I am working on? Check out my Personal Website and GitHub

Follow my blogs for Weekly new Tidbits on Dev

Items.Title: 什么是 SASS | 全时开发？ | What is SASS | All-time-dev?
Items.Description:
在这篇文章中，我们将了解什么是 SASS。 在这篇文章中，我们将了解它的历史以及 SASS/SCSS 开发人员的优势、劣势和薪水。 因此，如果您对我们的帖子感兴趣并喜欢我们的帖子，请与您的开发者朋友分享我们的帖子。

什么是 SASS，它的历史以及为什么要使用它？

它最初由 Hampton Catlin 设计，由 Natalie Weizenbaum 开发，之后 Eppstein 也加入了 Natalie Weizenbaum 并使用 SassScript 对其进行了扩展。

它于 2006 年 11 月 28 日首次出现，其稳定版本是 3.5.6，于 2018 年 3 月 23 日推出。它也被称为 SCSS，Web 开发人员也在使用它，它的教程可以在 YouTube 上以印地语免费获得，英语和其他语言，您还可以在 Udemy、GEEKS-FOR-GEEKS 和其他课程销售网站上找到它的课程。

我还会在我们的其他部分向您推荐一些视频。 它是用 Ruby 编写的，并被移植到其他流行的编程语言中，如 C++、JavaScript、JAVA 等。 它用于编译与浏览器兼容的 CSS，因为如果我们编写普通 CSS，则并非所有浏览器都支持它。

因此，为此，我们必须编写与所有浏览器（如 Apple SAFARI、IE 9 或 10 等）兼容的代码，在这种情况下，SASS 会将您编写的 CSS 编译为与所有浏览器兼容的 CSS 代码。 它还可以帮助您使用变量、嵌套规则，并且您可以通过安装一个名为 LIVE SASS COMPILER 的扩展轻松编写 SASS，这将帮助您实时观看 SASS，而无需不时保存您的 sass 文件并将您的代码编译成 CSS文本编辑器，如 Sublime、VS CODE 和 ATOM 等。

我还看到很多人对 SCSS、SASS 和 SassScript 有混淆。 有些人认为 SCSS 和 SASS 是不同的。 有些人认为它们是相同的，而 SassScript 是编写 SASS 的语言。 所以，所有这些都是错误的。

因此，如果您对它们之间有疑问，则可以清除。

所以，现在让我们讨论它们——

好处 -

  1. 它使您的应用程序与所有浏览器兼容
  2. 很容易学
  3. 它非常稳定和强大
  4. 它与所有版本的 CSS 兼容
  5. 帮助您编写干净且更少的代码。
  6. 帮助您使用变量、内联导入和其他功能。

缺点 -

  1. 可能导致浏览器内置元素检查器失去优势的原因
  2. 对开发人员进行困难的故障排除。
  3. 观看 CSS 之前必须编译代码。
  4. 需要互联网连接才能使用它。

学习 SASS 值不值得？

很多人也提出了 2021 年值得学习 SASS/SCSS 的问题。所以，我认为绝对值得学习不仅是 2021 年，而且对于未来也是因为所有企业都希望他们的电子商务商店或网站、博客等必须来自所有浏览器，因为他们想要更多的销售和用户，并且希望每个人都应该观看他们的产品，即使他是从 Safari、Chrome 或任何其他流行的网络浏览器观看的，并且开发人员希望他们必须编写更少的代码并且他们可以提供更多功能以通过他或她的工作吸引更多客户。

引用符号 ( & ) 您可能熟悉引用符号，它允许您像这样引用父元素： .block { &.red { color: red; 部分和@import 指令。

  • 插值。
  • 所以，我肯定会推荐使用 SASS。 如果您正在经营一家企业并想雇用开发人员，那么必须找到已经了解 SASS 的人，或者如果您是一名想要学习对您有帮助的新东西的开发人员。

    学习 SASS 的最佳 YouTube 视频

    来自 Free Code Camp 的 SASS 教程（学分）

    视频可在此处找到视频，当您滚动时，您会找到它。

    在本视频中，他们向我们介绍了 SASS 的基本知识，例如如何通过 Live SASS 编译器使用 SASS、如何创建 SASS 文件、如何通过创建网站设计来实际使用 SASS 的变量、mixin 和其他有用的功能等等. 因此，如果您有兴趣并想详细了解 SASS，那么您也可以访问他们的频道。

    来自设计课程 YouTube 频道的 SASS 课程（学分）

    在这个视频中，他展示了如何在一个小时内使用 SASS 并创建了一个漂亮的网页设计。 他提供了有关变量、嵌套、mixin 以及如何使用 Live SASS 编译器使用 SASS 的所有基本信息。 所以，如果你想快速学习 SASS，那么你可以观看他的视频。



    • 原文:

    In this post, we will find out what is SASS. In this post we will find out about its history and what are the advantages, disadvantages, and salary of a SASS/SCSS developer. So, if you are interested and like our posts then share our posts with your developer friends.

    What is SASS, its history and why is it used?

    SASS is a preprocessor scripting language and it mainly has two syntaxes one is the original syntax and the other one is the indented syntax which uses a similar syntax like HAML. It was initially designed by Hampton Catlin and developed by Natalie Weizenbaum and after that Eppstein also joined Natalie Weizenbaum and extended it using SassScript.

    It first appeared on November 28, 2006, and its stable version was 3.5.6 which was launched on March 23, 2018. It is also known as SCSS and web developers are also using it and its tutorials are freely available on YouTube in Hindi, English, and other languages and you can also find its courses on Udemy, GEEKS-FOR-GEEKS, and other course selling sites.

    I will also recommend you some videos in our other section. It is written in Ruby and ported in other popular programming languages like C++, JavaScript, JAVA, and more. The full form of SASS is (short for syntactically awesome style sheets). It is used to compile browser compatible CSS because if we write normal CSS then it is not supported by all browsers.

    So, for that, we have to write code that will be compatible with all browsers like Apple SAFARI, IE 9 or 10, etc and in that case, SASS compiles your written CSS into CSS code compatible for all browsers. And it also helps you to use variables, nested rules and you can write SASS easily by installing an extension called LIVE SASS COMPILER that will help you watch SASS live without saving your sass file time-to-time and compiles your code into CSS in all text editors like Sublime, VS CODE and ATOM, etc.

    Difference between SASS, SCSS, and SassScript?

    I have also seen that many people have confusion between SCSS, SASS, and SassScript. Some people believe that SCSS and SASS are different. Some people believe they are the same and SassScript is the language in which SASS is written. So, all of these are wrong.

    SASS is just a preprocessor scripting language and SCSS is the main syntax of SASS and SassScript is itself a scripting language that is used to compute CSS selector, property, or value using Sass expression. So, if you have a doubt between them then it is cleared.

    Advantages and Disadvantages of SASS

    SASS is a very good scripting language and it has both advantages and disadvantages. So, now let's discuss them -

    Advantages -

    1. It makes your app compatible with all browsers
    2. It is very easy to learn
    3. It is very stable and powerful
    4. It is compatible with all versions of CSS
    5. Helps you write clean and less code.
    6. Helps you use variables, inline imports, and other functionalities.

    Disadvantages -

    1. May Cause of losing benefits of browser's built-in element inspector
    2. Difficult Troubleshooting for developers.
    3. Code must be compiled before watching CSS.
    4. Need an internet connection for using it.

    Learning SASS is worth it or not?

    Many people also ask questions that are worth learning SASS/SCSS in 2021. So, I think that it is definitely worth learning not only 2021 but for the future also because all the businesses want that their e-commerce stores or websites, blogs, etc must be from all browsers because they want more sales and users and want that everyone should watch their products even if he is watching it from Safari, Chrome or any other popular web browser and the developers want that they have to write less code and they can give more functionalities to attract more clients by his or her work.

    SASS makes your website, e-commerce store or etc compatible for all popular browsers that a website owner wants that his or her website should everyone view and it provides you functionalities of a mixin that will help you to re-use a code that you have already written in that.

    CSS page and variables that will help you write less and there are many more features that will help a developer while creating a website like -

    1. Reference symbol ( & ) You might be familiar with the reference symbol, which allows you to reference a parent element as such: .block { &.red { color: red; } }
    2. Partials and @import directive.
    3. Interpolation.
    4. @content directive.

    So, I will definitely recommend using SASS. Either if you are running a business and want to hire developers then must find out someone who already knows SASS or if you are a developer who wants to learn something new that will help you.

    Best YouTube videos to learn SASS

    SASS tutorial from Free Code Camp (Credits)

    videos are available here video while you scroll you will find it.

    In this video, they give us the basic knowledge of SASS like How to use SASS using Live SASS compiler, how to create a SASS file, how to use variables, mixins, and other helpful features of SASS practically by creating a website design and more. So, if you are interested and want to learn about SASS in detail then you can also visit their channel.

    SASS course from design course YouTube channel (credits)

    In this video, he showed using SASS in one hour and also created a beautiful web design. He gave all the basic info about variables, nested, mixin, and how to use SASS using the Live SASS compiler. So, if you want to learn SASS fast then you can view his video.
Items.Author: alltimedevlopment

Items.Title: 编写 XSLT 的第一步 | Your First Step To Write XSLT
Items.Description:
为什么要使用 XSLT 🤔

我们可以在进行一些修改后将 XML 文件转换为另一个 XML 文件，我们也可以将 XML 文件转换为 HTML 页面，比如这个例子，我们在一个类别中有一些产品，我们需要用这些产品创建一个表。

 <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <category> <product> <name>watch</name> <price>50.60$</price> </product> <product> <name>IPhone 13</name> <price>5055.999$</price> </product> <product> <name>wallet</name> <price>5.10$</price> </product> </category>

这只是使用 XSLT 代码完成的，所以让我们深入了解一下 XSLT 👨‍💻

元素

我们有一些可以在 xslt 语言中使用的元素，您可以在参考资料中找到其中的大部分

顺便说一句，在 <> 符号范围内的元素旁边写的任何单词都称为属性，因此如果我需要为每个产品添加 id，我可以像这样将 id 属性放在每个产品元素中。

 <product id=1> <name>watch</name> <price>50.60$</price> </product>

但是如何使用这些元素以及如何告诉编辑器包含这些元素并识别它们，所有这些问题的答案都是这种语言的命名空间。

命名空间

您应该在 xslt 文件中写入的第一件事是命名空间，您可以用两种不同的方式编写它，但最后的结果相同。

 <xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">
 <xsl:transform version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">

变量

我们可以像往常一样在编程语言中声明全局变量或局部变量，但这里的区别在于，一旦设置了变量的值，就无法修改或更改该值，因此在 Java、PHP 和 Dart 等语言中将其视为最终变量，让我们继续看看如何声明一个变量

<xsl:variable name="name" select="expression"> </xsl:variable>
 <xsl:variable name="color" select="'red'" />

变量的名称可以在 name 属性中设置，变量的值也可以在 select 属性中设置，但是我们在这里有一个约束，如果变量的值是一个字符串，例如示例中的红色，我们应该把它放在引号中，而不仅仅是把价值。

循环

用其他语言编写一个 for 循环非常容易，只需在特定条件下编写 for 在这里它也非常相似，但使用不同的关键字xsl:for-each所以它会是这样。

 <xsl:for-each select="expression"> <!-- Content --> </xsl:for-each>

我们将在选择属性中放入我们需要循环的元素的路径，就像在我们的类别中一样 - 产品示例我们应该像这样放置产品的路径

<xsl:for-each select="category/product">. <!-- Content --> </xsl:for-each>

所以现在在这一点上，我们已经准备好遍历所有产品，最后一步是获取产品名称和价格以将它们放入表中，但是如果我们只需要通过检查来显示具有特定价格的产品怎么样价格是否高于或小于或等于任意值？ 当然如你所想，所以让我们去吃一顿非常美味的饭菜😅。

条件

我们有两种方法可以像往常一样设置第一个条件 if。

 <xsl:if test="expression"> ...some output if the expression is true... </xsl:if>

我们现在知道 test 是属性，我们应该把我们的条件而不是表达式放在 test 属性中，
但与往常不同的是，我们有用于比较的常量符号

- > -> means greater than - < -> means less than - = -> means equal - != -> means not equal

就像我们需要价格高于 10 美元的产品一样，这样的事情可以通过使用循环和条件来完成，如图所示。

 <xsl:for-each select="category/product"> <xsl:if test="price > 10"> <!-- Content --> </xsl:if> </xsl:for-each>

第二种方式就像 xslt 中其他语言中的 switch case 称为选择 - 何时。

 <xsl:choose> <xsl:when test="expression"> <!-- Content 1--> </xsl:when> <xsl:otherwise> <!-- Content 2 --> </xsl:otherwise> </xsl:choose>

我们也可以根据需要添加 when 语句，现在让我们实现产品的条件，但是选择 - when。

 <xsl:for-each select="category/product"> <xsl:choose> <xsl:when test="price > 10"> <!-- display product --> </xsl:when> <xsl:otherwise> <!-- hide product --> </xsl:otherwise> </xsl:choose> </xsl:for-each>

但是如果我们要在不同的地方编写相同的代码来做一些逻辑，但是以一种典型的方式，是的，你是对的，我们能做些什么，让我们去做吧💪

职能

在c++中编写函数你需要写它的名字和参数以及它的返回值类型，这里我们只需要名字和参数，所以你可以返回你需要的任何类型。

 <xsl:function name="functionName"> <xsl:param name="param1"/> <xsl:param name="param2"/> <xsl:value-of select="return value"/> </xsl:function>

所以很容易声明一个函数，也很容易像那样调用它。

 <xsl:value-of select="functionName('param1','param2')"/>

你能告诉我这个功能是做什么的吗？

 <xsl:function name="arbitraryName"> <xsl:param name="param1"/> <xsl:param name="param2"/> <xsl:value-of select="$param1 + param2"/> </xsl:function> <xsl:value-of select="arbitraryName('param1','param2')"/>

是的，你是对的，只需添加 2 个数字。
太好了，恭喜你🎉你现在准备好看看我们如何编写将 xml 转换为 html 表的文件。

但是在前一步，你还记得我们总是写在 if 的 body 和 for 的 body 中的 "<!--Content --" 吗？ 好的，太好了，如果我们需要将 xml 文件转换为 html 或 xml 元素，我们可以用我们想要的 html 标签替换这个注释，你想象你现在会在代码中看到什么吗？ 是的，我将添加一些构建表格的 html 标签，并在标签中添加一些 CSS 样式，这样就很容易了，伙计😃。

 <!-- namespace and version --> <?xml version="1.0"?> <xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"> <!-- access the root element --> <xsl:template match="/"> <html> <body> <h2 align="center">My Products Collection</h2> <table border="1" align="center"> <tr bgcolor="#9acd32"> <th>Name</th> <th>Price</th> </tr> <!-- access the path to products --> <xsl:for-each select="categoryproduct"> <!-- display all product without any conditions --> <tr> <td><xsl:value-of select="name"/></td> <td><xsl:value-of select="price"/></td> </tr> </xsl:for-each> </table> </body> </html> </xsl:template> </xsl:stylesheet>

结论😃

我们对 Xslt 所做的事情我们也可以对任何编程语言做，但在某些情况下，选择 xslt 比使用您的语言编码更强大和优化。



原文:

The XSLT is the abbreviation of eXtensible Stylesheet Language Transformation
xslt is a language of transforming XML documents so we can consider it as a style sheet for XML like CSS is a style sheet for HTML, also the XSLT files extensions end with .xslt or .xsl each of them is a valid extension.

Why to use XSLT 🤔

we can transform XML file to another XML file after making some modifications, also we have the ability to convert XML file to HTML page, like this example we have some products in a category and we need to create a table with these products.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<category>
<product>
     <name>watch</name>
     <price>50.60$</price>
</product>
<product>
      <name>IPhone 13</name>
      <price>5055.999$</price>
</product>
<product>
      <name>wallet</name>
      <price>5.10$</price>
</product>
</category>

that was done just with XSLT code, so lets go a little bit deep in XSLT 👨‍💻

XSLT Basic Architecture 🧱

Elements

We have some element that we can use in xslt language you can find most of them in the reference

for-each and value-of are called elements, so as a conclusion every param after xsl: is called element

<xsl:for-each select="expression">
  <!-- Content -->
</xsl:for-each>
<xsl:value-of select="expression" disable-output-escaping="yes|no" />

and by the way any word written beside the element within its scope in <> signs is called attribute so if i need to add id for each product i can put id attribute in each product element like that.

<product id=1>
     <name>watch</name>
     <price>50.60$</price>
</product>

but how to use these elements and how to tell the editor to include these elements and recognize them, all of these questions's answer is namespace of this language.

Namespace

The first thing you should write in xslt file is the namespace and you can write it with two different ways but with the same result at the end.

<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0"
xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">

<xsl:transform version="1.0"
xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">

xsl:stylesheet and xsl:transform are completely identical in the functions

Variables

we can declare as usual in programming languages global or local variable but the difference here is once you set the variable's value you can't modify or change that value so it is consider as final in language like Java, PHP and Dart, let's go forward to see how to declare a variable

<xsl:variable name="name" select="expression"> </xsl:variable>

<xsl:variable name="color" select="'red'" />

the name of variable can be set in the name attribute also the value of the variable can be set in select attribute but we have one constrain here that we should put variable's value if it will be a string in quotes like red in the example not just put the value.

Loops

Its very easy in other language to write a for loop just write for with specific condition also here its a quite similar but with different keyword xsl:for-each so it will be like that.

<xsl:for-each select="expression">
  <!-- Content -->
</xsl:for-each>

We will put in the select attribute the path of element that we need to loop in like in our category - product example we should put the path of products like that

<xsl:for-each select="category/product">.
  <!-- Content -->
</xsl:for-each>

so now in this point we are ready to loop over all products with the final step to get the name of the product and the price to put them into table, but how about if we need to just show the product with specific price by checking the price if it is higher than or less than or equal to an arbitrary value? of-course as you thought, so lets go and eat a very delicious meal conditions 😅.

Conditions

We have two ways to make a conditions the first one like usual if.

<xsl:if test="expression">
  ...some output if the expression is true...
</xsl:if>

as we know now test is attribute, we should put our condition instead of expression in the test attribute,
but as not usual we have constant symbols for comparing

- >  -> means greater than
- <  -> means less than
-  =    -> means equal
- !=    -> means not equal

like if we need products which prices are greater than 10$, so stuff like that can be done by using loops and conditions together as shown.

<xsl:for-each select="category/product">
      <xsl:if test="price > 10">
             <!-- Content -->
      </xsl:if>
    </xsl:for-each>

the second way is like switch case in other languages in xslt called choose - when.

<xsl:choose>
  <xsl:when test="expression">
             <!-- Content 1-->
  </xsl:when>
  <xsl:otherwise>
             <!-- Content 2 -->
  </xsl:otherwise>
</xsl:choose>

also we can add when statements as we need, now lets implement the condition of products but with choose - when.

<xsl:for-each select="category/product">
      <xsl:choose>
        <xsl:when test="price > 10">
                      <!-- display product -->
        </xsl:when>
        <xsl:otherwise>
                      <!-- hide product -->
        </xsl:otherwise>
      </xsl:choose>
 </xsl:for-each>

but what can we do if we will write the same code in different places to make some logic but in a typical way, yes you are right use functions, lets do it 💪

Functions

In c++ to write function you need to write its name and parameters also its return value type, here we just need name and parameter so you can return any type you need.

 <xsl:function name="functionName">
    <xsl:param name="param1"/>
    <xsl:param name="param2"/>
    <xsl:value-of select="return value"/>
  </xsl:function>

so it is very easy to declare a function also it is very easy to call it like that.

<xsl:value-of select="functionName('param1','param2')"/>

can you tell me what does this function do?

 <xsl:function name="arbitraryName">
    <xsl:param name="param1"/>
    <xsl:param name="param2"/>
    <xsl:value-of select="$param1 + param2"/>
  </xsl:function>

<xsl:value-of select="arbitraryName('param1','param2')"/>

yes you are right just add 2 numbers.
Great let me tell you congratulations 🎉 you are now ready to see how could we write the file that convert xml to html tables.

but one step before, do you remember "<!--Content --" that we always wrote in if's body also in for's body? ok great, we could replace this comment with what we want like html tags if we need to convert xml file to html or xml elements whatever want, do you imagine what you will see now in the code? yes exactly just i will add some html tags that construct the tables and put some CSS styling in the tag so it will be easy peasy dude 😃.

<!-- namespace and version -->
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0"
xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform">


<!-- access the root element  -->
<xsl:template match="/">
  <html>
    <body>
      <h2 align="center">My Products Collection</h2>
        <table border="1" align="center">
          <tr bgcolor="#9acd32">
             <th>Name</th>
             <th>Price</th>
          </tr>

<!-- access the path to products  -->
       <xsl:for-each select="categoryproduct">

<!-- display all product without any conditions  -->
       <tr>
         <td><xsl:value-of select="name"/></td>
         <td><xsl:value-of select="price"/></td>
       </tr>
      </xsl:for-each>
    </table>
  </body>
  </html>
</xsl:template>

</xsl:stylesheet>

Conclusion 😃

What we did with Xslt we can also did with any programming language but in some cases choice of xslt is more powerful and optimized than coding with your language.
Items.Author: engineermostafa

Items.Title: 时事通讯 martinmueller.dev 2022 第 19 周 | Newsletter martinmueller.dev 2022 week 19
Items.Description:
在这份每周时事通讯中，我写了一周内引起我注意的帖子的摘要。 我以 AWS、DevOps、架构、AWS CDK、数据工程、数据分析等主题为中心。

如果您喜欢我的内容，请在我经常闲逛的地方关注我：

使用 AWS 无服务器的简单休假管理系统

使用 AWS Step Functions 构建像解释的简单休假管理这样的工作流程非常酷。 此外，最近发布的AWS 开发工具包服务集成使定义此类变得有趣。

非常感谢作者Pubudu Jayawardana

又是一个不错的每周总结。 我的亮点是aws-dataall ，因为它看起来非常有趣，可以在内部共享 ML 数据公司并在外部共享事件 mhh。 这里有很多值得思考的地方。 但与往常一样，还有大量其他黄金，所以你必须探索它！

再次感谢作者Ricardo Sueiras 。 我希望你喜欢柏林峰会^^。 下次算我一个！

简单的无服务器调度程序

一篇很酷的文章，介绍了如何解决一个有趣的商业案例，例如当用户取消订阅但仍然需要在付费期结束之前访问。 作者比较了如何实现这种无服务器调度程序的不同可能解决方案。

非常感谢作者Pawel Zubkiewicz

使用新的控制台体验调试 AWS Step Functions 执行

您可以调试 Step Functions 的能力对我来说变得非常重要，本文非常适合了解更多信息。 迫不及待想亲自尝试一下！

非常感谢作者本杰明·史密斯。

无服务器威胁建模第 2 部分 🚀

关于无服务器威胁建模的一个很好的自上而下的帖子。

非常感谢作者Lee James Gilmore

架构模式

对单体架构、N 层架构、面向服务的架构、基于消息的架构、基于微服务的架构、反应式架构和无服务器架构的架构模式进行了很好的总结。

非常感谢作者dev0928

红移vs雅典娜

关于 AWS Redshift 与 Athena 的有趣讨论。 远离 AWS Step Functions 的 3 个理由

非常好地深入了解 AWS Step Functions。 太酷了，作者从 Step Functions 初学者、中级和高级的角度描述了一些问题以及如何解决这些问题。 好主意！ 必读！

非常感谢作者Allen Helton

超级有趣的话题是关于 IT 领域女性人数减少以及女性在 IT 领域获得认可有多难。 不幸的是它是德语，但您可以使用翻译服务:)。

非常感谢作者Andreas Weck

关于 DevOps：1. 它是什么

关于 DevOps 和 DevOps 文化的有趣讨论。 喜欢它是什么等等。 以及在这种情况下，领导力意味着什么。

非常感谢作者郭铁心

关于定价、何时使用、限制等方面的 AWS Lambda 深入探讨。 它甚至解决了冷启动问题，并就如何最大程度地减少这种负担提供了很好的建议。 超酷！

非常感谢作者Tobias Schmidt

使用 AWS 自动化机器学习生命周期

与机器学习相关的有用 AWS 服务的非常广泛的列表。 虽然我不相信阅读只需要 11 分钟 :D !!

非常感谢作者罗伯特约翰

一篇关于如何使用 SSO SAML 和命名配置文件进行 AWS CDK 部署的有趣文章。 非常感谢作者Alessandro Gaggia

如何在 2022 年获得远程软件开发人员工作 | 高薪| 国际| 远程工作

包含有关如何获得远程开发人员工作的非常好的提示。

非常感谢作者每天学习和成长

保护 AWS Lambda 函数 URL

关于 AWS Lambda 函数网址中的漏洞的精彩综述

非常感谢作者阿米泰科恩

使用 Crowd Sentry 的 AWS 安全最佳实践

https://blog.crowdsentry.io/aws-security-best-practices-with-crowd-sentry-b3605f1854bc

关于一些 AWS 安全最佳实践以及如何保留这些实践的简短摘要。

非常感谢作者Mike Wade

再放大

一个我觉得非常有趣的开源 CDK 社区项目。 它正在从 AWS Amplify UI 和 AWS CDK 中挑选用于部署。 我也在我的私人项目中这样做，例如https://github.com/senjuns/senjuns 。 我认为作者可以通过使用https://github.com/projen/projen进行项目设置来进一步增强/简化其 repo。

非常感谢作者托马斯鲁尼

在您的 AWS CDK 部署的 Lambda 函数中安全地使用密钥

说明如何使用 AWS Secrets Manager 存储与您的 CDK 部署相关的密钥。 该示例是用 Python 编写的 :)。

非常感谢作者Wesley Cheek

结对编程如何帮助团队

我发现结对编程的话题非常有趣。 我从来没有机会这样做，但我会改变它！ 这篇文章很好地总结了为什么结对编程很好。 它还提供了有关如何进行结对编程的详细信息。

非常感谢作者迈克尔·罗萨里奥

如何成为一名优秀的开发者？

说出了我的心声。

非常感谢作者MD Sarfaraj

无服务器应用程序：为什么 IaC 应该是方向

一篇关于为什么使用基础设施即代码 (IAC) 的好文章。

非常感谢作者Jones Zachariah Noel N

最后的话

感谢那些精彩帖子的作者。 并感谢时事通讯的读者。 如果您喜欢这种格式或知道如何改进它，请告诉我:)。 让我们建造吧！

我喜欢从事开源项目。 我的很多东西你已经可以在https://github.com/mmuller88上使用了。 如果您喜欢我在那里的工作和我的博客文章，请考虑支持我：

或者

别忘了访问我的网站



原文:

In this weekly newsletter, I write a summary of posts that got my attention during the week. I center around topics AWS, DevOps, Architecture, AWS CDK, Data Engineering, Data Analytics and much more.

If you like my content follow me on my usual spots to hang around:

Simple Leave Management System with AWS Serverless

https://dev.to/aws-builders/simple-leave-management-system-with-aws-serverless-3dnp

Constructing a workflow like the explained simple leave management with AWS Step Functions is so cool. Furthermore, the recently released AWS SDK service integrations makes it fun to define such.

Thanks a lot to the author Pubudu Jayawardana

AWS open-source news and updates, #112

https://dev.to/aws/aws-open-source-news-and-updates-112-5bjp

And again a nice weekly summary. My highlight is the aws-dataall as it looks super interesting for sharing ML data company internally and event externally mhh. Lots to think about here. But as always there is tons of other gold so you have to explore that!

Thanks again to the author Ricardo Sueiras. I hope you enjoyed the Summit in Berlin ^^. Next time count me in!

Simple Serverless Scheduler

https://dev.to/aws-builders/simple-serverless-scheduler-25c7

A cool article for how to solve an interesting business case like when a user cancels their subscription but still needs to have access until the end of the paying period time. The author compared different possible solutions for how to implement such a serverless scheduler.

Thanks a lot to the author Pawel Zubkiewicz

Debugging AWS Step Functions executions with the new console experience

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/compute/debugging-aws-step-functions-executions-with-the-new-console-experience/

The capability that you can debug Step Functions became super important to me and this article is great to learn more about it. The new debug AWS Console experience for Step Functions looks super exciting. Can't wait to try it myself!

Thanks a lot to the author Benjamin Smith.

Serverless Threat Modelling Part 2 🚀

https://leejamesgilmore.medium.com/serverless-threat-modelling-part-2-87e69d447fa8

A nice top-down post on serverless threat modeling.

Thanks a lot to the author Lee James Gilmore

Architectural Patterns

https://dev.to/dev0928/architectural-patterns-5f31

Nice roundup of the architectural patterns Monolith Architecture, N-tier Architecture, Service-oriented Architecture, Message-based Architecture, Microservice-based Architecture, Reactive Architecture, and Serverless Architecture.

Thanks a lot to the author dev0928

Redshift vs Athena

https://www.reddit.com/r/aws/comments/umx793/redshift_vs_athena/

Interesting discussion about AWS Redshift vs Athena. Athena seems to keep the costs low as it is serverless but it would be rather slow when you need a quick answering kind of API.

3 Reasons You Should Stay Away From AWS Step Functions

https://www.readysetcloud.io/blog/allen.helton/when-not-to-use-step-functions/

Very nice deep dive into AWS Step Functions. It is so cool that the author describes some problems and how to solve them from a view as a Step Functions Beginner, Intermediate and Advanced. Nice idea! Must read!

Thanks a lot to the author Allen Helton

Annahita Esmailzadeh: „Ich entspreche nicht dem Stereotyp eines IT-lers“

https://t3n.de/news/annahita-esmailzadeh-stereotype-it-tech-branche-1471342/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=feed&utm_campaign=news

Super interesting talk about fewer women in IT and how hard it is for women to be acknowledged in the IT area. Unlucky it is in German but you can use a translation service :).

Thanks a lot to the author Andreas Weck

On DevOps: 1. What It Is

https://dev.to/devstream/on-devops-1-what-it-is-3lmd

Interesting discussion about DevOps and DevOps Culture. Like what it is and more. As well as in this context what does leadership mean.

Thanks a lot to the author Tiexin Guo

Starter Guide & “Things I wish I knew before” for AWS Lambda

https://towardsaws.com/starter-guide-things-i-wish-i-knew-before-for-aws-lambda-fe36ce2fd4cb

A nice deep dive into AWS Lambda regarding pricing, when to use it, limitations and much more. It even tackles the cold start problem and gives good advice on how to minimize that burden. Super cool!

Thanks a lot to the author Tobias Schmidt

Automating machine learning lifecycle with AWS

https://trojrobert.medium.com/automating-machine-learning-lifecycle-on-aws-trojrobert-385a66b82fbf

A very broad listing of useful AWS services related to machine learning. Though I don't believe that it shall only take 11 minutes to read :D !!

Thanks a lot to the author Robert John

Shortcut for AWS CDK credentials: insanely simple setup for SSO, SAML, and named profiles

https://dev.to/aws-builders/shortcut-for-aws-cdk-credentials-insanely-simple-setup-for-sso-saml-and-named-profiles-35a9

An interesting post on how to use SSO SAML and named Profiles for AWS CDK deployments. TBH I never used CDK deploy --profile $PROFILE_NAME but yeah if I have to this article explains it in very good detail.

Thanks a lot to the author Alessandro Gaggia

How to get remote software developer jobs in 2022 | high paying | international | remote job

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ftC-jQgmcwI

Contains very good tips for how to get a remote developer job.

Thanks a lot to the author Learn And Grow Everyday

Securing AWS Lambda function URLs

https://www.wiz.io/blog/securing-aws-lambda-function-urls

Nice roundup about what are vulnerabilities from AWS Lambda Function Urls

Thanks a lot to the author Amitai Cohen

AWS Security Best Practices with Crowd Sentry

https://blog.crowdsentry.io/aws-security-best-practices-with-crowd-sentry-b3605f1854bc

A nice short summary about some AWS Security Best Practices and how to keep those.

Thanks a lot to the author Mike Wade

Reamplify

https://github.com/thomasrooney/reamplify

An Open Source CDK Community project which I find super interesting. It is doing cherry-picking from AWS Amplify UI and AWS CDK for deployment. I do that in my private projects as well for example https://github.com/senjuns/senjuns. I think the author could enhance/simplify its repo even more by using https://github.com/projen/projen for the project setup.

Thanks a lot to the author Thomas Rooney

Securely Use Secrets In Your AWS CDK Deployed Lambda Function

https://dev.to/wesleycheek/securely-use-secrets-in-your-aws-cdk-deployed-lambda-function-1ebb

Explains how to use AWS Secrets Manager for storing secrets related to your CDK deployments. And the example is written in Python :).

Thanks a lot to the author Wesley Cheek

How Pair Programming Helps The Team

https://dev.to/michaelprosario/how-pair-programming-helps-the-team-3045

I find the topic of pair programming super interesting. I never had the opportunity to do that but I will change that! This article is a very good roundup of why pair programming is nice. As well it gives details on how to do pair programming.

Thanks a lot to the author Michael Rosario

How to become a good developer?

https://dev.to/this-is-learning/how-to-become-a-good-developer-7no

Speaks to the bottom of my heart.

Thanks a lot to the author MD Sarfaraj

Serverless apps: Why IaC should be the direction

https://zachjonesnoel.hashnode.dev/serverless-apps-why-iac-should-be-the-direction

A nice article on why to use Infrastructure as Code (IAC).

Thanks a lot to the author Jones Zachariah Noel N

Final Words

Thank you to the authors of those amazing posts. And thank you to the readers of the newsletter. When you like this format or know how to improve it please let me know :). Let's Build!

I love to work on Open Source projects. A lot of my stuff you can already use on https://github.com/mmuller88 . If you like my work there and my blog posts, please consider supporting me on:

OR

And don't forget to visit my site
Items.Author: mmuller88

Items.Title: React-Native 重要提示 | React-Native Top Tips
Items.Description:
你好呀 ！！

我带着一个新博客回来了。 所以这次我将介绍一些关于 React-Native 的有趣技巧，这是一个 JavaScript 框架。
所以这次让我们和🐱 Suga 一起去吧：

在进一步讨论之前，让我们先讨论一下 React-Native 是什么？ 它的用途是什么？

反应原生：

重要提示：

不参加世博会：我知道说“不要参加世博会”是不明智的。 但这就是现实。 从世博开始是好的，但坚持下去，不是。 几乎所有的科技巨头都不会选择 Expo，因为它处理大型项目的能力并不强。 此外，社区套餐也非常少。

从您的发布版本中删除所有日志：我认为在这个世界上，不存在不使用 console.log() 进行调试的开发人员。 此外，它是一个非常强大的工具，但同时如果在打包或发布 App 时忘记移除控制台，那么它会降低我们的 App 的速度。

使用 flex ：如果想要我们的 App 高效运行，那么必须依赖flex而不是过度使用
使用尺寸的高度和宽度。 使用 Dimension 很好，因为它使我们的应用程序响应，但同时依赖它是非常痛苦的，因为它会降低应用程序的效率，因为每次使用它都会计算整个屏幕的大小并根据我们的选择应用高度和宽度这会增加内存使用率，从而降低效率。

重用样式：重用样式在 React-Native 中是一种很好的做法，可以减少重复的代码行。 例如，在每个屏幕中，我们都为起始视图赋予了一些样式。 因此，在每个屏幕中，而不是一次又一次地键入该视图的样式，应该创建一个通用样式 JavaScript 文件并在我们需要的任何地方使用它。 我们还可以为颜色、字体大小、卡片样式制作不同的文件，这些文件将在整个应用程序中重复使用。

简洁地使用三元运算符：写颜色=错误？ 书写颜色 = 错误？ 三元运算符可以帮助减少代码中的行数，但不要嵌套它们，因为逻辑变得难以阅读。



原文:

Hi there !!

I am back with a new Blog. So this time I will cover some interesting tips about React-Native which is a framework of JavaScript.
So let's go with 🐱 Suga this time :

React-Native Top Tips :

Before moving further, let's first discuss what React-Native is ? And what is its use ?

React-Native :

React Native is an open-source UI software framework created by Meta Platforms, Inc. It is used to develop applications for Android, iOS, Web, Windows etc. by enabling developers to use the React framework along with native platform capabilities.

Top Tips :

Don’t go with Expo : I know it's not wise to say 'Don't use Expo'. But this is the actual reality. Starting with Expo is good, but stick to it, not. Almost all tech giants don't go with Expo as it's not that much capable to handle large projects. Also, community packages also very less.

Remove all logs from your release builds : I think in this world, there is no such developer exist who don't use console.log() for debugging. Also, it's very powerful tool but at the same time if one forget to remove console while packaging or releasing App then it will slow down our App.

Use flex : If one want's that our App works efficiently, then one must rely on flex instead of excessively using
Height and Width using Dimensions. Using Dimension is good as it makes our App responsive but at the same time dependent on it is very painful as it will decrease the efficiency of App as every time it used it calculate the size of whole screen and apply height and width according to our selection which increase memory usage and thus efficiency decreases.

Reuse Styles : Reusing styles is a good practice in React-Native and reduce the repetitive code lines. For example, in every screen we are giving some style to Starting View. So in every screen instead of typing styles of that View again and again one should create a common style JavaScript file and use it everywhere we needed. We can also make different file for Colors, font Size, Card Style which will be reused in the entire App.

Use ternary operators concisely : Writing color = error ? ‘red’: ‘gray’ is short and sweet. Writing color = error ? (id === ID) ? ‘red’ : ‘black’ : ‘gray’ is not.

Ternary operators can help reduce the number of lines in your code, but never nest them because the logic becomes difficult to read.

I'm a very lazy person, and always procrastinate on the smallest of things - opening the image editor, and making a good blog banner would always be the "extra" thing I had to do every time I wrote something. So, I made a script that generates them for me in milliseconds!

As Bill Gates says,

always choose a lazy person to do a difficult job because he will find an easy way to do it

The output is fully customizable, as it is 100% generated from code. You can change some values here and there, and boom! Generated. I ran it through all the posts of my blog and it quickly replaced many of the blog posts for me.

Check out the code Here (And ⭐ the post if you liked it!)

Installation

Config

The default config works well, but if you want your banner to be a different colour, or change the positioning of elements, feel free to edit the config file

Running the script

You can run the script by using the generate() method of the ImageFactory class

if __name__ == "__main__" generator = ImageFactory() generator.generate( ... # Options here

title, description…

Items.Title: 在 Remix 中添加 Markdown 插件 | Adding Markdown plugins in Remix
Items.Description:
现在我们已经启动并运行了基于Remix Markdown 的网站，让我们来探索如何通过添加一些很酷的 Markdown 插件来增强它。

对于本文，我们将添加目录 Rehype 插件。

安装 Rehype 插件

我们必须首先将插件添加到我们的项目中。 要使用目录插件，我们还需要安装 slug 和自动链接标题插件，因为它依赖于这些插件。 

 npm i rehype-autolink-headings rehype-to rehype-slug

安装这些后，我们必须在 Remix 中注册它们。 幸运的是，Remix 带有一个remix.config.js文件。

我们可以在这个文件中添加一个mdx部分并加载我们的插件。

 module . exports = { // Existing lines, mdx : async ( filename ) => { const [ rehypeSlug , rehypeAutolinkHeadings , rehypeToc ] = await Promise . all ([ import ( ' rehype-slug ' ). then (( mod ) => mod . default ), import ( ' rehype-autolink-headings ' ). then (( mod ) => mod . default ), import ( ' rehype-toc ' ). then (( mod ) => mod . default ), ]); return { rehypePlugins : [ rehypeSlug , rehypeAutolinkHeadings , rehypeToc ], }; }, };

现在我们已经加载了所有三个插件，它已经可以工作了！ 无需在我们的降价中添加任何其他内容。
我们可以通过运行 outcode 来尝试一下，看看会发生什么。

您还可以在GitHub 上找到完整的代码。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

Now that we have our Remix Markdown powered website up and running let's explore how we can enhance it a bit by adding some cool Markdown plugins.

For this article, we'll add the table of contents Rehype plugin.

Installing the Rehype plugin

We have to start by adding the plugin to our project. To use the table of contents plugin, we also need to install the slug and auto-link headings plugins as it relies on these.

npm i rehype-autolink-headings rehype-to rehype-slug

With these installed, we have to register them in Remix. Luckily Remix comes prepared with a remix.config.js file.

We can add an mdx section in this file and load our plugins.

module.exports = { // Existing lines, mdx: async (filename) => { const [rehypeSlug, rehypeAutolinkHeadings, rehypeToc] = await Promise.all([ import('rehype-slug').then((mod) => mod.default), import('rehype-autolink-headings').then((mod) => mod.default), import('rehype-toc').then((mod) => mod.default), ]); return { rehypePlugins: [rehypeSlug, rehypeAutolinkHeadings, rehypeToc], }; },
};

Now that we have loaded all three plugins, it will already work! No need to add anything else to our markdown.
We can try it out by running outcode and see what happens.

You can also find the complete code on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

Items.Title: springboot注解@Cacheable自定义文件设置key设置过期时间并在配置中配置 | Springboot annotation @Cacheable custom file set key to set expiration time and configure it in the configuration
Items.Description:
目的和效果

使用@Cacheable 注解时，默认为redisCache。 通过在配置文件中设置不同key的过期时间，可以达到自定义key过期时间的效果。
计划
第1步

创建一个新的 Map 类来存储要设置的键

@ConfigurationProperties public class Properties { private final Map < String , Duration > initCaches = Maps . newHashMap (); public Map < String , Duration > getInitCaches () { return initCaches ; } }

第2步

在redis配置类中写cacheManager，把map set放进去

@Autowired private Properties properties ; @Bean public CacheManager cacheManager ( RedisConnectionFactory connectionFactory ) { RedisCacheConfiguration defaultCacheConfig = RedisCacheConfiguration . defaultCacheConfig () . entryTtl ( Duration . ofMinutes ( 10 )). disableCachingNullValues (); RedisCacheWriter redisCacheWriter = RedisCacheWriter . nonLockingRedisCacheWriter ( connectionFactory ); ImmutableSet . Builder < String > cacheNames = ImmutableSet . builder (); ImmutableMap . Builder < String , RedisCacheConfiguration > cacheConfig = ImmutableMap . builder (); for ( Map . Entry < String , Duration > entry : properties . getInitCaches (). entrySet ()) { cacheNames . add ( entry . getKey ()); cacheConfig . put ( entry . getKey (), defaultCacheConfig . entryTtl ( entry . getValue ())); } return RedisCacheManager . builder ( redisCacheWriter ) . cacheDefaults ( defaultCacheConfig ) . initialCacheNames ( cacheNames . build ()) . withInitialCacheConfigurations ( cacheConfig . build ()) . build (); }

第三步

通过在Springboot yml文件中配置相关key的过期时间，可以指定@Cacheable的值的过期时间

//Fake code @Cacheable ( cacheNames = "fooboo" , key = "#xxx" , unless = "#result == null" ) public void fooboo ( String xxx ){}

在 applicaion.yml 文件中设置

initCaches: fooboo: 10m fooboo1: 1h

那么redis缓存中fooboo的值10分钟后过期，fooboo1的值1小时后过期



原文:

The configuration of the Springboot RedisCacheManager class specifies the expiration time of the key and configures it in the configuration file

purpose and effect

RedisCache is configured in springBoot. When the @Cacheable annotation is used, the default is redisCache. By setting the expiration time of different keys in the configuration file, the effect of customizing the key expiration time can be achieved.
plan
step1

Create a new Map class to store the key to be set

@ConfigurationProperties
public class Properties { private final Map<String, Duration> initCaches = Maps.newHashMap(); public Map<String, Duration> getInitCaches() { return initCaches; }
}

step 2

Write the cacheManager in the redis configuration class and put the map set into it

@Autowired
private Properties properties; @Bean
public CacheManager cacheManager(RedisConnectionFactory connectionFactory) { RedisCacheConfiguration defaultCacheConfig = RedisCacheConfiguration.defaultCacheConfig() .entryTtl(Duration.ofMinutes(10)).disableCachingNullValues(); RedisCacheWriter redisCacheWriter = RedisCacheWriter.nonLockingRedisCacheWriter(connectionFactory); ImmutableSet.Builder<String> cacheNames = ImmutableSet.builder(); ImmutableMap.Builder<String, RedisCacheConfiguration> cacheConfig = ImmutableMap.builder(); for (Map.Entry<String, Duration> entry : properties.getInitCaches().entrySet()) { cacheNames.add(entry.getKey()); cacheConfig.put(entry.getKey(), defaultCacheConfig.entryTtl(entry.getValue())); } return RedisCacheManager.builder(redisCacheWriter) .cacheDefaults(defaultCacheConfig) .initialCacheNames(cacheNames.build()) .withInitialCacheConfigurations(cacheConfig.build()) .build();
}

step3

By configuring the expiration time of the relevant key in the Springboot yml file, you can specify the expiration time of the value of @Cacheable

//Fake code
@Cacheable(cacheNames = "fooboo", key = "#xxx", unless = "#result == null")
public void fooboo(String xxx){}

set in the applicaion.yml file

initCaches:
  fooboo: 10m
  fooboo1: 1h

Then the value of fooboo in the redis cache expires in 10 minutes, and the value of fooboo1 expires in 1 hour

Items.Title: 初级开发人员必读的 4 本书 | 4 Must-Reads for the Junior Developer
Items.Description:
作为软件工程师的职业可能会令人困惑，如果您是新手，更是如此。 这里有 5 本书可以帮助理解这个复杂的问题。

此列表中唯一一本并非严格针对软件工程师的书。 一篇长而全面的读物，涵盖了许多对规划你的职业生涯有用的策略和技巧。 这本有声读物由 John Sonmez（作者）亲自讲述，在您的通勤途中很容易阅读。 这本书涵盖了您可能面临的许多问题，例如学习哪种语言，如何填补您的技术知识“较软”主题中的空白，例如面试时穿什么。

受到许多人的高度评价，并且是任何软件工程师阅读清单上最重要的建议之一。 我每隔几年就会重读这本书，它给我带来了新的见解，每次都证明它是一颗宝石。

虽然它不是最全面的，但它确实使它更有趣，特别是如果您没有计算机科学背景！ 我建议使用这本书作为对该主题更正式研究的介绍。

可能是我拥有的最长的一本书。 类似于The Complete Software Developer's Career Guide - John Sonmez的范围，但侧重于技术方面的求职以及如何为它做准备。 涵盖的主题包括面试后应该问的问题，以及重要数据结构和算法的应用。



原文:

A career as a Software Engineer can be confusing, moreso if you're new. Here are 5 books to help make sense of this complex problem.

1. So Good They Can't Ignore You - Cal Newport

The only book on this list not strictly catered for Software Engineers. Cal Newport writes about the mindset of taking control of your life, providing research-backed evidence for learning to love what you do.

2. The Complete Software Developer's Career Guide - John Sonmez

A long, but comprehensive read that covers many of the strategies and techniques useful to planning your career. The audio book was narrated by John Sonmez (the author) himself and is easy to consume on your commute. The book covers many questions you might face such as which language to learn, how to fill gaps in your technical knowledge "softer" topics such as what to wear to interviews.

3. The Pragmatic Programmer - David Thomas, Andrew Hunt

Viewed in high regard by many and one of the top recommendations on any Software Engineer's reading list. The Pragmatic Programmer highlights many common pitfalls and best practices that will help you approach technical challenges. I reread this book every few years and it brings me new insight proves to be a gem every time.

4. Grokking Algorithms

Grokking Algorithms is a very accessible way to learn data structures and algorithms. While it isn't the most comprehensive, it certainly makes it more enjoyable, especially if you do not have a background in Computer Science! I would recommend using this book as an introduction into more formal studies on the subject.

5. Cracking the Coding Interview

Probably the longest book I own. Similar to The Complete Software Developer's Career Guide - John Sonmez in scope, but with a focus on the technical aspect job hunt and how to prepare for it. Covers topics such as questions you should ask after an interview, and exerts of important data structures and algorithms.

Items.Title: 什么是坏代码以及如何处理它 | What is bad code and what to do about it
Items.Description:
糟糕的代码就像糟糕的修复。 它可以工作，甚至可以工作，但它很糟糕，而且时间不长，它看起来一般，而且要修复它并不容易。 它很混乱，结构难以理解，你不会立即明白作者想说什么。

代码质量差的迹象：

  • 很难在其中导航；

  • 能写成两行的部分写成十行；

  • 通过函数的名称无法理解它的作用（xyz，function，n）；

  • 如果你添加一些东西，其余的将停止工作。

是什么威胁着糟糕代码的项目

乍一看，糟糕的代码并不是一场灾难。 该程序有效，这不是主要的吗？ 事实上，它带来了很多问题，尤其是从长远来看。 这就是为什么：

  • 对低质量代码进行更改需要大量时间且成本高昂，而且随着时间的推移，价格和持续时间只会增加；

  • 添加新东西也很困难；

  • 代码是不可预测的——你永远不知道什么会停止工作以及什么时候停止工作；

  • 如果人们不得不使用这样的杰作，团队精神就会下降（“这到底是谁写的？！”）。

结果，开发速度降低，时间线变长，变更成本越来越高。 如果这对消费者或客户至关重要，那么糟糕的代码可能会掩埋项目。

如何写出好的代码

要编写高质量的代码，您需要知道它是什么。 以下是它的主要品质：

  • 代码易于阅读，结构清晰，没有混淆；

  • 它易于维护：小的变化不会引起再次编写它的愿望；

  • 它可以扩展；

  • 代码已经过测试；

  • 记录在案；

  • 富有成效的。

注释对于使代码易于阅读非常重要。 这不仅会在其他程序员处理您的代码时有所帮助，而且在未来也会对您有所帮助。

以下是改进代码的五个操作。

这是最简单和最重要的准则之一——当您事先与同事达成一致时，如何格式化代码以及如何在某些情况下采取行动。 那么不同程序员编写的代码在视觉上会很相似：你使用别人的代码会更容易，反之亦然。 还有普遍接受的编码标准，甚至编程平台会告诉你如何遵循这些标准。 这在团队工作和单独编写代码时都很重要。

这是在不改变其行为的情况下对程序代码的改造。 也就是说，首先你有一个创造性的过程：你编写以便程序工作，然后你“自己清理”，把代码按顺序排列成想要的样子。 作为奖励，重构有助于重新思考程序的架构决策。

让我们用一个真实的例子来解释。 玩家写信给一款游戏的开发者关于问题，然后突然停止。 起初，开发人员很高兴：一切都非常酷，没有任何问题。 后来发现用户名在游戏中变成了昵称，因此，反馈表停止工作：投诉根本没有发送。 键入（声明数据类型）将有助于避免此类错误并简化维护和重构。

这是您和同事检查彼此的代码并互相提供反馈的时候。 这种做法有助于避免愚蠢的错误和粗心大意。 此外（这也很重要），您知道其他人在做什么，并且您不必复制功能。 一个美妙的奖励 - 这种方法将有助于温和地适应初学者。

这些是运行具有不同参数的代码并检查已知结果的简单程序。 有哪些测试？

  • 对程序的各个部分进行单元测试。

  • 加载器检查程序处理负载的能力。

  • 屏幕截图比较屏幕截图前后的变化，并对变化做出反应。

  • 其他：突变、渗透……

对于测试，您确实需要小心，以免您花费数小时寻找代码中的错误，但问题在于测试本身。 因此，当您对需要做什么和想要得到什么有清晰的结构和理解时，先编写测试然后再编写代码本身是很有用的。

还有其他改进代码的方法（例如，结对编程）。 如果一次使用多种方法可以获得最佳结果：这样更可靠。

当好代码坏时

我们一直在谈论质量代码的重要性，现在我们说的恰恰相反？ 是的。 有时好的、“正确”的代码并不是那么好。
原因很简单：不幸的是，在编程中，您必须在工作质量和速度之间做出选择，而在某些情况下，速度更重要。

要了解速度的重要性，请想象两个开发人员：John 和 Steve。 一是彻底的、迂腐的，二是完全相反的。 两人都想出了制造某种产品的想法。 约翰坐下来，想出架构并开始慢慢地做好它。 并且已经做了六个月了。 史蒂夫在一个月内完成了所有事情，有错误，有错误 - 但立即启动它，找到客户，发布第二个版本......结果，六个月后，当约翰刚刚完成他的精彩产品时，史蒂夫已经拥有很多用户并且该产品的名称已经众所周知。 他修复了第二个版本中的一些错误。

故事的寓意：有时更快地进入市场并占据一席之地比做得完美更重要。

那该怎样呢？ 糟糕的代码是糟糕的，好的代码也不总是好的......重点是：

代码质量是一个参数
我们根据目标而改变。

如果您正在为银行编写应用程序，那么您将花费大量时间进行测试和开发，因为一个错误可能会造成数十万美元的损失。

如果您正在制作一个简单地讲述临时促销并将电子邮件收集到数据库的登录页面，那么速度在这里就已经很重要了。 您知道，一旦促销结束，您的代码将不再需要。 不需要细化和维护它，其他开发者也不会使用它——快速编写并检查它是否有效就足够了，其余的不重要。

这些是创建高质量代码的基本原则：不要忘记标准，回顾你已经编写的内容，不要害怕同事的反馈，不要忘记测试。 但一开始还是值得权衡的：现在质量对你来说很重要，或者你需要尽快完成开发。



原文:

Bad code is like bad repair. It can work and even works, but it’s bad and not for long, it looks so-so and it’s not easy to fix something in it. It is chaotic, has an incomprehensible structure, and you won’t immediately understand what the author wanted to say.

Signs of poor quality code:

  • it is difficult to navigate in it;

  • parts that can be written in two lines are written in ten;

  • by the name of the function it is impossible to understand what it does (xyz, function, n);

  • if you add something to it, the rest will stop working.

What threatens a project with bad code

At first glance, bad code is not a disaster. The program works, isn't that the main thing? In fact, it brings many problems, especially in the long run. And that's why:

  • making changes to low-quality code takes a lot of time and is expensive, and over time, the price and duration only increase;

  • adding something new is also difficult;

  • code is unpredictable - you never know what will stop working and when;

  • team spirit falls if people have to work with such masterpieces ("Who wrote this at all?!").

As a result, the development speed is reduced, the timeline is lengthened, and the cost of changes is growing. If this is critical for the consumer or customer, then bad code can bury the project.

How to write good code

To write quality code, you need to know what it is. Here are its main qualities:

  • the code is easy to read, it has a clear structure, there is no confusion in it;

  • it is easily maintained: small changes do not cause a desire to write it again;

  • it can be expanded;

  • the code has been tested;

  • it is documented;

  • productive.

Comments are really very important to make the code easy to read. This will not only help other programmers when they work on your code, but also you in the future.

Here are five actions that improve the code.

1. Follow coding standards. This is one of the simplest and most important guidelines - when you agree with colleagues in advance how to format the code and how to act in some situations. Then the code written by different programmers will be visually similar: it will be easier for you to work with someone else's code, and vice versa. There are also generally accepted coding standards and even programming platforms that will tell you how to follow these standards. This is important both when working in a team and if you write code alone.

2. Refactoring. This is a reworking of the program code without changing its behavior. That is, first you have a creative process: you write so that the program works, and then you “clean up after yourself”, put the code in order, to the desired look. As a bonus, refactoring helps to rethink the architectural decisions of the program.

3. Typing. Let's explain with a real example. Players wrote to the developers of one game about problems, and then suddenly stopped. At first, the developers were delighted: everything is so cool that there are no problems. Later it turned out that the username had changed to a nickname in the game, and because of this, the feedback form stopped working: complaints were simply not sent. Typing (declaring a data type) will help avoid such errors and simplify maintenance and refactoring.

4. Code review. This is when you and a colleague check each other's code and give each other feedback. This practice helps to avoid stupid mistakes and thoughtlessness. In addition (and this is also important), you are aware of what the other person is doing, and you do not have to duplicate functions. A wonderful bonus - this method will help to gently adapt beginners.

5. Automatic tests we will talk about them in a little more detail. These are simple programs that run code with different parameters and check against already known results. What are the tests?

  • Unit test individual parts of the program.

  • e2e test the system as a whole.

  • Loaders check how well the program handles loads.

  • Screenshots compare before and after screenshots and react to changes.

  • Others: mutational, penetration…

With tests, you really need to be careful so that it doesn’t turn out that you spend hours looking for an error in the code, but the problem is in the tests themselves. Therefore, it is useful to write tests first, and then take on the code itself, when you have a clear structure and understanding in your head of what needs to be done and what you want to get.

There are other ways to improve the code (for example, pair programming). The best results can be obtained if you use several methods at once: this is more reliable.

When good code is bad

We've been talking so much about how important quality code is now, and now we're saying the opposite? Yes. Sometimes good, "correct" code isn't all that great.
The reason is very simple: in programming, unfortunately, you have to choose between quality and speed of work, and in some cases speed is more important.

To understand how important speed is, imagine two developers: John and Steve. One is thorough, pedantic, and the second is its complete opposite. Both came up with the idea to make some kind of product. John sat down, came up with the architecture and started to do it well and slowly. And has been doing it for six months now. Steve did everything in a month, with errors, bugs - but immediately launched it, found customers, made the second release... As a result, six months later, when John had just completed his wonderful product, Steve already has many users and the name of the product is already well known. And he fixed some of the bugs in the second release.

Moral of the story: sometimes it's more important to get to the market faster and occupy a niche than to do it perfectly.

How then to be? Bad code is bad, good code is not always good either ... The point is this:

Code quality is a parameter that
we change depending on the goal.

If you are writing an application for a bank, then you will spend a lot of time testing and developing, because a mistake can cost hundreds of thousands of dollars.

If you are making a landing page that simply tells about a temporary promotion and collects emails into a database, then speed is already important here. You know that once the promotion ends, your code will no longer be needed. There is no need to refine and maintain it, and other developers will not use it either - it is enough to write quickly and check that it works, the rest is not important.

These are the basic principles of creating quality code: do not forget about standards, look back at what you have already written, do not be afraid of feedback from colleagues and do not forget to test. But at the very beginning, it is worth weighing: quality is important to you now, or you need to finish development as soon as possible.
